hypothesis: Incorporating DFT+U corrections specifically parameterized for the identified problematic 3d transition metals (Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni) as an intermediate fidelity level within a multi-fidelity ∆-learning hierarchy—where the first ∆ corrects GFN1-xTB to DFT+U and the second ∆ corrects DFT+U to HSE06—will yield greater band gap prediction accuracy for transition-metal-containing MOFs than the current two-level GFN1-xTB-to-HSE06 ∆-learning scheme, because the intermediate level explicitly captures strong d-electron correlation effects that GFN1-xTB cannot describe, thereby reducing the magnitude and variance of the correction that the ML model must learn.
